revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''黒岩横穴墓群'''（くろいわよこあなぼぐん）とは、[[埼玉県]][[比企郡]][[吉見町]]にある横穴式の集合[[墓|墳墓]]である。埼玉県指定[[史跡]]。
==特徴== [[凝灰岩]]の岩山の斜面に多数の穴が空いている。古墳時代中～後期に造られたものと推定されているが、学術的な調査がほとんどされていないため、詳細は不明。
地元では「十六穴」と呼ばれることもある。これは、[[1877年]]（明治10年）、地元の有志により、初めて16個の横穴が発見・発掘されたことに由来する。現在では、30個以上の穴の存在が確認されている。
近くにある[[吉見百穴]]よりも大規模で、約500個以上の穴があると推測されている。さらに、ほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴より良好と考えられている。今後の発掘調査が待たれる。
==その他== *黒岩横穴墓群の約2.5km南西には、同様の遺跡である「[[吉見百穴]]」がある。こちらはほぼ発掘が終わっており、観光施設として有料公開されている。岩山に多数の穴が開いた異様な姿を見ることができ、また貴重な植物である[[ヒカリゴケ]]の自生地としても知られている。
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[日本の史跡一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/guide_kuroiwayokoana.html 吉見町サイト内の黒岩横穴墓群紹介ページ]
{{history-stub}} {{japan-geo-stub}} [[Category:古墳|くろいわよこあなほくん]] [[Category:史跡|くろいわよこあなほくん]] [[Category:埼玉県の歴史|くろいわよこあなほくん]] [[Category:埼玉県の建築物・観光名所|くろいわよこあなほくん]]

==特徴== [[凝灰岩]]の岩山の斜面に多数の穴が空いている。古墳時代中～後期に造られたものと推定されているが、学術的な調査がほとんどされていないため、詳細は不明。
地元では「十六穴」と呼ばれることもある。これは、[[1877年]]（明治10年）、地元の有志により、初めて16個の横穴が発見・発掘されたことに由来する。現在では、30個以上の穴の存在が確認されている。

'''黒岩横穴墓群'''（くろいわよこあなぼぐん、“くろいわ'''おうけつ'''ぼぐん”とも読む）とは、[[埼玉県]][[比企郡]][[吉見町]]にある横穴式の集合[[墓|墳墓]]である。[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|埼玉県指定史跡]]。
==特徴== 灌漑用の人工沼である[[八丁湖]]の北側、公園のはずれにある。[[凝灰岩]]の岩山の斜面に多数の穴が空いている。古墳時代中～後期に造られたものと推定されているが、学術的な調査がほとんどなされていないため、詳細は不明。
地元では「十六穴」と呼ばれることもある。これは、[[1877年]]（明治10年）に地元の有志により、初めて16個の横穴が発見・発掘されたことに由来する。現在では、30個以上の穴の存在が確認されている。
観光施設ではなく、公園とは低い柵で仕切られているだけなので、穴がある岩山には容易に近づくことができる。ただし、十分な管理がなされているわけではなく、季節によっては周囲に草木が生い茂っていたりするので、接近して観察する場合は十分な注意が必要である（蛇やムカデ、毒虫などがいる恐れがある）。
近くにある[[吉見百穴]]よりも大規模で、500個以上の穴があるものと推測されている。さらに、そのほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴より良好と考えられている。今後の[[発掘調査]]が待たれる。
==その他== *黒岩横穴墓群の約2.5km南西には、同様の遺跡である「[[吉見百穴]]」がある。こちらはほぼ発掘が終わっており、観光施設として有料公開されている。岩山に多数の穴が空いた異様な姿を見ることができ、また貴重な植物である[[ヒカリゴケ]]の自生地としても知られている。
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/guide_kuroiwayokoana.html 吉見町サイト内の黒岩横穴墓群紹介ページ]

地元では「十六穴」と呼ばれることもある。これは、[[1877年]]（明治10年）に地元の有志により、初めて16個の横穴が発見・発掘されたことに由来する。現在では、30個以上の穴の存在が確認されている。
観光施設ではなく、公園とは低い柵で仕切られているだけなので、穴がある岩山には容易に近づくことができる。ただし、十分な管理がなされているわけではなく、季節によっては周囲に草木が生い茂る。
約2.5km南西には、同様の遺跡である[[吉見百穴]]がある。黒岩横穴墓群は吉見百穴よりも大規模で、500個以上の穴があるものと推測されている。さらに、そのほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴より良好と考えられている。

'''黒岩横穴墓群'''（くろいわよこあなぼぐん、くろいわおうけつぼぐん）とは、[[埼玉県]][[比企郡]][[吉見町]]にある横穴式の集合[[墓|墳墓]]である。[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|埼玉県指定史跡]]。
==特徴== 灌漑用の人工沼である[[八丁湖]]の北側、公園のはずれにある。[[凝灰岩]]の岩山の斜面に多数の穴が空いている。古墳時代中～後期に造られたものと推定されているが、学術的な調査がほとんどなされていないため、詳細は不明。
地元では「十六穴」と呼ばれることもある。これは、[[1877年]]（明治10年）に地元の有志により、初めて16個の横穴が発見・発掘されたことに由来する。現在では、30個以上の穴の存在が確認されている。
観光施設ではなく、公園とは低い柵で仕切られているだけなので、穴がある岩山には容易に近づくことができる。ただし、十分な管理がなされているわけではなく、季節によっては周囲に草木が生い茂る。
約2.5km南西には、著名な群集墳[[吉見百穴]]があり、一連のものである可能性が高い。黒岩横穴墓群は吉見百穴よりも大規模で、500個以上の穴があるのではないかという推測もあるが、十分な踏査はなされていない。さらに、そのほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴より良好と考えられている。

地元では「十六穴」と呼ばれることもある。これは、[[1877年]]（明治10年）に地元の有志により、初めて16個の横穴が発見・発掘されたことに由来する。現在では、30個以上の穴の存在が確認されている。
観光施設ではなく、公園とは低い柵で仕切られているだけなので、穴がある岩山には容易に近づくことができる。ただし、十分な管理がなされているわけではなく、季節によっては周囲に草木が生い茂る。
約2.5km南西には、著名な[[群集墳]]で国指定[[史跡]][[吉見百穴]]がある。黒岩横穴墓群は吉見百穴よりも大規模で、500個以上の穴があるのではないかと推測されているが、十分な調査はなされていない。また、そのほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴に比べきわめて良好と考えられている。

約2.5km南西には、著名な[[群集墳]]で国指定[[史跡]][[吉見百穴]]がある。黒岩横穴墓群は吉見百穴よりも大規模で、500個以上の穴があるのではないかと推測されているが、十分な調査はなされていない。また、そのほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴に比べきわめて良好と考えられている。

観光施設ではなく、公園とは低い柵で仕切られているだけなので、穴がある岩山には容易に近づくことができる。ただし、十分な管理がなされているわけではなく、季節によっては周囲に草木が生い茂る。
約2.5km南西には、著名な[[群集墳]]の[[吉見百穴]]（国の[[史跡]]）がある。黒岩横穴墓群は吉見百穴よりも大規模で、500個以上の穴があるのではないかと推測されているが、十分な調査はなされていない。また、そのほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴に比べきわめて良好と考えられている。
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/guide_kuroiwayokoana.html 吉見町サイト内の黒岩横穴墓群紹介ページ]

[[ファイル:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|right|thumb|300px|黒岩横穴墓群（2001年8月）]] '''黒岩横穴墓群'''（くろいわよこあなぼぐん、くろいわおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[比企郡]][[吉見町]]にある [[横穴墓|横穴墓群]](横穴式の集合[[墓|墳墓]])である。[[1925年]][[3月31日]]、[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|埼玉県の史跡]]に指定。
==概要== 灌漑用の人工沼である[[八丁湖]]の北側、公園のはずれにある。[[凝灰岩]]の岩山の斜面に多数の穴が空いている。古墳時代中～後期に造られたものと推定されているが、学術的な調査がほとんどなされていないため、詳細は不明である。
地元では「十六穴」と呼ばれることもある。これは、[[1877年]]（明治10年）に地元の有志により、初めて16個の横穴が発見・発掘されたことに由来する。現在では、30個以上の穴の存在が確認されている。
観光施設ではなく、公園とは低い柵で仕切られているだけなので、穴がある岩山には容易に近づくことができる。ただし、十分な管理がなされているわけではなく、季節によっては周囲に草木が生い茂る。
約2.5km南西には、著名な[[群集墳]]の[[吉見百穴]]（国の[[史跡]]）がある。黒岩横穴墓群は吉見百穴よりも大規模で、500個以上の穴があるのではないかと推測されているが、十分な調査はなされていない。また、そのほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴に比べきわめて良好と考えられている。
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/guide_kuroiwayokoana.html 吉見町サイト内の黒岩横穴墓群紹介ページ]

revision.comment: Created page with " == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. === Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site..."
revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|ja|清戸迫横穴墓}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|ja|双葉町}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|ja|泉崎横穴墓}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|ja|西白河郡}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|ja|泉崎村}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|ja|中田横穴}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|ja|羽山横穴}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|和田大仏及び横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.

About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. '''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.

Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group]

It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. '''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokouana Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster" to "Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster"
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Removing this excess]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Burial Mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]
