'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|12=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{citation needed|Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|12=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{citation needed|Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group}}

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])

== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|12=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{citation needed|Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group}}

== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|12=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|12=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{citation needed|Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group}}

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|12=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{citation needed|Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group}}

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])

== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|12=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{citation needed|Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group}}

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:黒岩横穴墓群 up to rev 98356028
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =黒岩横穴墓群 |別名 = |画像 =[[ファイル:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|250px]] |画像キャプション =黒岩横穴墓群（2001年8月） |所在地 =[[埼玉県]][[比企郡]][[吉見町]] |規模 =推定500基以上 |所属 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|42.0|N|139|26|19.7|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} |築造年代=[[6世紀]]-[[7世紀]] |形状 =[[横穴墓]] |史跡指定 =[[1925年]]（大正14年）[[3月31日]]県指定{{Cite web|和書|author=生涯学習課文化財係|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=黒岩横穴墓群|work=吉見町|accessdate=2020-08-01}} |登録文化財 = |出土品の文化財指定 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=黒岩
横穴墓群|lat_deg=36|lat_min=03|lat_sec=42.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=26|lon_sec=19.7|mark=横穴墓.svg|marksize=30|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=埼玉県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|03|42.0|N|139|26|19.7|E}}}} }} '''黒岩横穴墓群'''（くろいわよこあなぼぐん/くろいわおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[比企郡]][[吉見町]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。[[1925年]]（大正14年）[[3月31日]]に埼玉県の[[史跡]]に指定された。 ==概要== [[灌漑]]用の人工沼である[[八丁湖]]の北側、公園のはずれにある。[[凝灰岩]]の岩山の斜面に多数の横穴が開穴している。[[古墳時代]]後期-終末期に造られたものと推定されているが、学術的な調査がほとんどなされていないため、詳細は不明である。 地元では「十六穴」と呼ばれることもある。これは、[[1877年]]（明治10年）に地元の有志により、初めて16基の横穴が発見・発掘されたことに由来する。現在では、30基以上の横穴の存在が確認されている。 観光施設ではなく、公園とは低い柵で仕切られているだけなので、横穴がある岩山には容易に近づくことができる。ただし、十分な管理がなされているわけではなく、季節によっては周囲に草木が生い茂る。 約2.5キロメートル南西には、著名な横穴墓群の[[吉見百穴]]（国の[[史跡]]）がある。黒岩横穴墓群は吉見百穴よりも大規模で、500基以上の横穴があるのではないかと推測されているが、十分な調査はなされていない。また、そのほとんどが未発掘のため、保存状態は吉見百穴に比べて良好と考えられている。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡]] ==外部リンク== *[https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html 吉見町サイト内の黒岩横穴墓群紹介ページ] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{japan-geo-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くろいわよこあなほくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:埼玉県指定史跡]] [[Category:吉見町の歴史]]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|12=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japan-geo-stub}}

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])

== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hyakuana|qq=}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster]] → [[Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hyakuana|qq=}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hyakuana|qq=}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hyakuana|qq=}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Final Kofun period|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hyakuana|qq=}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Final Kofun period]] → [[Late Kofun tumuli]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hyakuana|qq=}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakuana|ja|吉見百穴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hyakuana|qq=}}, a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:吉見町の歴史
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

<!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:吉見町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:群集墳]]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
