'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.

Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Historic Sites of Saitama Prefecture
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Yoshimi Town
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.

Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "町の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:吉見の Municipal History]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:群集墳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.

Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:吉見の Municipal History]] into [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshimi]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Cultural Properties of Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

== External Links == * [https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html Yoshimi Town site - Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group]

'''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|qid=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|qid=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshimi]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|qid=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|qid=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|qid=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshimi]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11678129}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|qq=|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
