revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Municipal History of Yoshimi]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11678129}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in [[:en:1877|1877]] (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known [[:en:Yoshimi Hundred Caves|Yoshimi Hyakuana]], a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11678129]] Wikidata name: Kuroiwa Burial Caves [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "Cave Tomb" to "Yokoanabo"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
== References == {{Reflist}}

It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana|1=Yoshimi Hundred Caves}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313|1=Yoshimi, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana|1=Yoshimi Hundred Caves}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|WD=Q543313|1=Yoshimi, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|WD=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana|1=Yoshimi Hundred Caves}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|qid=Q543313|1=Yoshimi, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|qid=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|qid=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana|1=Yoshimi Hundred Caves}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Yoshimi Kuroiwa Tombs of the cave 1.JPG|location={{ill|Yoshimi|qid=Q543313|1=Yoshimi, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.061667|N|139.438806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (黒岩横穴墓群) is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Yoshimi, Hiki District, Saitama Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Saitama Prefecture on [[March 31, 1925]] (Taishō 14)<ref name=Saitama>{{Cite web|author=Shōgai Gakushūka Cultural Properties Section|url=https://www.town.yoshimi.saitama.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushuk/7/430.html|title=Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group|work=Yoshimi Town|accessdate=2020-08-01}}</ref>.
== Overview == The tomb group is located north of {{ill|Hatchō Lake|ja|八丁湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatchō Lake|1=Lake Hatchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11390455}}, an artificial reservoir used for irrigation, at the edge of a park. Many tomb openings are carved into the slopes of a tuff rock formation. The tombs are estimated to date from the [[Late Kofun period|late]] to [[Late Kofun tumuli|final Kofun period]]<ref name=Saitama/>; however, due to the lack of academic research, details remain unclear.
Locally, the tombs are sometimes called "Sixteen Holes" (十六穴), a name derived from the first excavation in {{ill|1877|en|1877|qid=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) by local volunteers, who uncovered and excavated 16 tombs. Today, more than 30 tombs have been confirmed.
Although not a tourist facility, the site is only separated from the park by a low fence, making it easily accessible. However, due to limited management, vegetation may overgrow in certain seasons.
About 2.5 kilometers southwest lies the well-known {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|qid=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hyakuana|1=Yoshimi Hundred Caves}}, a [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] of Japan. Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Tomb Group is considered larger than Yoshimi Hyakuana, with an estimated 500 or more tombs<ref name=Saitama/>. However, due to a lack of systematic excavation, the exact number remains uncertain. Since most tombs remain unexcavated, they are believed to be in better condition compared to Yoshimi Hyakuana.
